description:
To properly set the group owner of /var/log/*.journal(~), run the command: 
$ sudo chgrp systemd-journal /var/log/*.journal(~)
or 
$ sudo chgrp root /var/log/*.journal(~)
rationale: The <tt>/var/log/*.journal(~)</tt> files are system logs managed by the "systemd" service.
ocil_clause:
/var/log/*.journal(~) does not have a group owner of
systemd-journal or
root
ocil:
To check the group ownership of /var/log/*.journal(~), run the command: 
$ ls -lL /var/log/*.journal(~)
If properly configured, the output should indicate the following group-owner: systemd-journal or root
title: Verify Group Who Owns /var/log/*.journal(~) File
